title: Manual DNA Extraction using Qiagen DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit
author: Julie Haendiges
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bi4dkgs6
materials: Qiagen DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit
Discard the collection tube and its contents.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in the bacterial culture...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in the bacterial culture by pulse vortexing 3-5 times on low setting or by pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1.0 mL bacterial culture into a 2.0 ml sterile Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Pellet cells by centrifuging at 7500...
steps.instruction: Pellet cells by centrifuging at 7500 rpm, °C, for 00:05:00 minutes.

steps.title: Discard supernatant into a 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant into a 50 ml conical tube with 10% commercial bleach marked “biohazard waste".

steps.title: Select the appropriate lysing conditions based...
steps.instruction: Select the appropriate lysing conditions based on the type of bacteria:

steps.title: Prepare enzymatic lysis buffer (ELB) containing...
steps.instruction: Prepare enzymatic lysis buffer (ELB) containing 20 mM Tris-HCl, 2 mM EDTA, and 1.2% Triton. A 250ml stock will be prepared. This stock buffer will last for 1 year at room temperature. Mix 5 mL of 1 M Tris-HCl, 1 mL of 0.5 M EDTA, 3 mL of Triton X-100 and enough molecular-grade water to bring the volume up to 250 mL . Mix gently to incorporate the Triton.

steps.title: Add 180 µL l of Buffer ATL
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL l of Buffer ATL. (Check for precipitate in ATL, if precipitate is present, warm to 56 o C until it is fully dissolved)

steps.title: Label lid of a DNeasy spin...
steps.instruction: Label lid of a DNeasy spin column with a unique, recorded identifier for each sample.

steps.title: Using a P1000 pipette, transfer full...
steps.instruction: Using a P1000 pipette, transfer full volume of pre-treated bacterial cells to the corresponding spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (> 6000 x g) for 00:01:00

steps.title: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2
steps.instruction: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2.0 ml collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW1
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW1.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (> 6000 x g) for 00:01:00

steps.title: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2
steps.instruction: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2.0 ml collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW2
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW2.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13000 rpm, °C, (>20000 x g) for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13000 rpm, °C, (>20000 x g) for 00:03:00 .

steps.title: Examine the spin column to ensure...
steps.instruction: Examine the spin column to ensure that no liquid remains on the spin column. If there is liquid present, go to step #23 until it is clean.

steps.title: Place spin column in a new, sterile 1
steps.instruction: Place spin column in a new, sterile 1.5 ml elution tube labeled with a unique, recorded identifier and/or FDA accession (CFSANxxxxxx) number.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Buffer EB to the spin column
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Buffer EB to the spin column. Optional: Incubate Buffer EB at 37°C to improve elution.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>6000 x g) for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>6000 x g) for 00:01:00

steps.title: DO NOT discard eluate, it is the purified DNA
steps.instruction: DO NOT discard eluate, it is the purified DNA. DNA can be stored at 4 °C for short-term storage and -20 °C or -80 °C for long-term storage.

steps.title: Label lid of a DNeasy spin...
steps.instruction: Label lid of a DNeasy spin column with a unique, recorded identifier for each sample.

steps.title: Using a P1000 pipette, transfer full...
steps.instruction: Using a P1000 pipette, transfer full volume of pre-treated bacterial cells to the corresponding spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (> 6000 x g) for 00:01:00

steps.title: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2
steps.instruction: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2.0 ml collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW1
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW1.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (> 6000 x g) for 00:01:00

steps.title: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2
steps.instruction: Place DNeasy spin column into a new 2.0 ml collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW2
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer AW2.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13000 rpm, °C, (>20000 x g) for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13000 rpm, °C, (>20000 x g) for 00:03:00 .

steps.title: Examine the spin column to ensure...
steps.instruction: Examine the spin column to ensure that no liquid remains on the spin column. If there is liquid present, go to step #23 until it is clean.

steps.title: Place spin column in a new, sterile 1
steps.instruction: Place spin column in a new, sterile 1.5 ml elution tube labeled with a unique, recorded identifier and/or FDA accession (CFSANxxxxxx) number.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Buffer EB to the spin column
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Buffer EB to the spin column. Optional: Incubate Buffer EB at 37°C to improve elution.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>6000 x g) for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, (>6000 x g) for 00:01:00

steps.title: DO NOT discard eluate, it is the purified DNA
steps.instruction: DO NOT discard eluate, it is the purified DNA. DNA can be stored at 4 °C for short-term storage and -20 °C or -80 °C for long-term storage.

steps.title: Add 20 mg/ml of lysozyme to...
steps.instruction: Add 20 mg/ml of lysozyme to appropriate amount of lysis buffer within 24 hours of use. Note: for Staphyloccocus spp., add 1 mg of lysostaphin for every 2 ml of enzymatic lysis buffer, regardless of ELB preparation method.

steps.title: Add 180 µL of enzymatic lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL of enzymatic lysis buffer (ELB) + Enzyme to sample tube. Resuspend bacterial cell pellet by vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 25 µL of Proteinase K
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL of Proteinase K. Add 200 µL of Buffer AL. Vortex on high setting for 5 seconds to mix.

steps.title: Incubate at 56 °C for at least 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56 °C for at least 00:30:00

steps.title: Add 200 µL of 100% Ethanol and mix by vortexing
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of 100% Ethanol and mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K. Resuspend bacterial cell pellet by vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:30:00

steps.title: Add 4 µL of RNase A
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL of RNase A. Mix by vortexing and incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Add 200 µL of Buffer AL and mix by vortexing
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of Buffer AL and mix by vortexing. (Check for precipitate in AL, if precipitate is present, warm to 56 o C until it is fully dissolved)

steps.title: Add 200 µL of 100% ethanol and mix by vortexing
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of 100% ethanol and mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Add 4 µL of RNase A
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL of RNase A. Mix by vortexing and incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Removed the inoculated TSB samples from the incubator
steps.instruction: Removed the inoculated TSB samples from the incubator.
